member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.CopyFilePattern
member.summary: Represents a file pattern for copying dependency files in a Dockerfile.
member.param: The source pattern for files to copy (e.g., "package*.json").
The destination path where files should be copied (e.g., "./").

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.CopyFilePattern.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents a file pattern for copying dependency files in a Dockerfile.
member.param: The source pattern for files to copy (e.g., "package*.json").
The destination path where files should be copied (e.g., "./").

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.CopyFilePattern.Source
member.summary: The source pattern for files to copy (e.g., "package*.json").

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.CopyFilePattern.Destination
member.summary: The destination path where files should be copied (e.g., "./").

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptAppResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a JavaScript application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The command to execute.
The working directory to use for the command.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptAppResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: A resource that represents a JavaScript application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The command to execute.
The working directory to use for the command.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptBuildScriptAnnotation
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the JavaScript package manager's build script.
member.param: The name of the JavaScript package manager's build script.
The command line arguments for the JavaScript package manager's build script.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptBuildScriptAnnotation.#ctor(System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the JavaScript package manager's build script.
member.param: The name of the JavaScript package manager's build script.
The command line arguments for the JavaScript package manager's build script.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptBuildScriptAnnotation.ScriptName
member.summary: Gets the name of the script used to build.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptBuildScriptAnnotation.Args
member.summary: Gets the command-line arguments supplied to the build script.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptInstallCommandAnnotation
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the JavaScript package manager's install command.
member.param: The command line arguments for the JavaScript package manager's install command. This includes the command itself (i.e. "install").

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptInstallCommandAnnotation.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the JavaScript package manager's install command.
member.param: The command line arguments for the JavaScript package manager's install command. This includes the command itself (i.e. "install").

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptInstallCommandAnnotation.Args
member.summary: Gets the command-line arguments supplied to the JavaScript package manager.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptInstallCommandAnnotation.ProductionInstallArgs
member.summary: Gets or sets the additional arguments for installing production-only dependencies (excluding devDependencies). This flag is appended to the base install command (from ) when generating the production dependencies stage in the Dockerfile. Each package manager sets its own flag (e.g. npm uses --omit=dev, yarn uses --production, pnpm uses --prod).

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptInstallerResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a package installer for a JavaScript app.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The working directory to use for the command.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptInstallerResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: A resource that represents a package installer for a JavaScript app.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The working directory to use for the command.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageInstallerAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an annotation for a JavaScript installer resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageInstallerAnnotation.#ctor(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ExecutableResource)
member.summary: Represents an annotation for a JavaScript installer resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageInstallerAnnotation.Resource
member.summary: The instance of the Installer resource used.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the JavaScript package manager used in a resource.
member.param: The name of the executable used to run the package manager.
The command used to run a script with the JavaScript package manager.
The BuildKit cache mount path for the package manager, or null if not supported.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the JavaScript package manager used in a resource.
member.param: The name of the executable used to run the package manager.
The command used to run a script with the JavaScript package manager.
The BuildKit cache mount path for the package manager, or null if not supported.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation.ExecutableName
member.summary: Gets the executable used to run the JavaScript package manager.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation.ScriptCommand
member.summary: Gets the command used to run a script with the JavaScript package manager.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation.CommandSeparator
member.summary: Gets the string used to separate individual commands in a command sequence, or if one shouldn't be used. Defaults to "--".

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation.CacheMount
member.summary: Gets the BuildKit cache mount path for the package manager, or null if not supported.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation.PackageFilesPatterns
member.summary: Gets the file patterns for package dependency files.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptPackageManagerAnnotation.InitializeDockerBuildStage
member.summary: Gets or sets a callback to initialize the Docker build stage before installing packages.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptRunScriptAnnotation
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the script used during run mode in a JavaScript resource.
member.param: The name of the JavaScript package manager's run script.
The command line arguments for the JavaScript package manager's run script.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptRunScriptAnnotation.#ctor(System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the script used during run mode in a JavaScript resource.
member.param: The name of the JavaScript package manager's run script.
The command line arguments for the JavaScript package manager's run script.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptRunScriptAnnotation.ScriptName
member.summary: Gets the name of the script to run.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.JavaScriptRunScriptAnnotation.Args
member.summary: Gets the command-line arguments for the script.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.NextJsAppResource
member.summary: Represents a Next.js application resource.
member.param: The unique name used to identify the Next.js application resource.
The command to execute the application.
The working directory from which the application command is executed.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.NextJsAppResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents a Next.js application resource.
member.param: The unique name used to identify the Next.js application resource.
The command to execute the application.
The working directory from which the application command is executed.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.NodeAppResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a node application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The command to execute.
The working directory to use for the command.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.NodeAppResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: A resource that represents a node application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The command to execute.
The working directory to use for the command.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.PublishAsStaticWebsiteOptions
member.summary: Options for configuring the static website publish mode.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.PublishAsStaticWebsiteOptions.OutputPath
member.summary: Gets or sets the relative path to the directory containing the built static files. Defaults to dist. Some frameworks use a different output directory, for example Angular uses dist/browser.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.PublishAsStaticWebsiteOptions.StripPrefix
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to remove the API path prefix before forwarding to the backend. For example, with apiPath="/api" and StripPrefix=true, a request to /api/weatherforecast is forwarded as /weatherforecast. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.PublishAsStaticWebsiteOptions.TargetEndpointName
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of a specific endpoint on the API target resource to proxy to. When , YARP uses service discovery to resolve the appropriate endpoint, preferring HTTPS when available.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.ViteAppResource
member.summary: Represents a Vite application resource that can be managed and executed within a Node.js environment.
member.param: The unique name used to identify the Vite application resource.
The command to execute the Vite application, such as the script or entry point.
The working directory from which the Vite application command is executed.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.ViteAppResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents a Vite application resource that can be managed and executed within a Node.js environment.
member.param: The unique name used to identify the Vite application resource.
The command to execute the Vite application, such as the script or entry point.
The working directory from which the Vite application command is executed.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.ViteAppResource.ViteConfigPath
member.summary: Gets or sets the path to the Vite configuration file.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for adding JavaScript applications to an .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.AddNodeApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a node application to the application model. Node should be available on the PATH.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the resource.
The path to the directory containing the node application.
The path to the script relative to the app directory to run.
member.remarks:
This method executes a Node script directly using node script.js. If you want to use a package manager you can add one and configure the install and run scripts using the provided extension methods.
If the application directory contains a package.json file, npm will be added as the default package manager.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.AddJavaScriptApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a JavaScript application resource to the distributed application using the specified app directory and run script.
member.param: The distributed application builder to which the JavaScript application resource will be added.
The unique name of the JavaScript application resource. Cannot be null or empty.
The path to the directory containing the JavaScript application.
The name of the npm script to run when starting the application. Defaults to "dev". Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A resource builder for the newly added JavaScript application resource.
member.remarks: If a Dockerfile does not exist in the application's directory, one will be generated automatically when publishing. The method configures the resource with Node.js defaults and sets up npm integration.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.PublishAsStaticWebsite``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.PublishAsStaticWebsiteOptions})
member.summary: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a standalone static website served by YARP.
member.param: The JavaScript resource builder.
Optional callback to configure .

remarks.para: The published container uses a YARP reverse proxy image for static file serving. To add an API reverse-proxy, use the overload that accepts an apiPath and apiTarget.

member.summary: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a standalone static website served by YARP, with an API reverse-proxy to the specified resource.
member.param: The JavaScript resource builder.
A path prefix to reverse-proxy to a backend API. For example, /api proxies all requests matching /api/{"{**catch-all}"} to the backend resource.
The backend resource to proxy API requests to. YARP uses service discovery to resolve the appropriate endpoint, preferring HTTPS when available.
Optional callback to configure .

remarks.para: The published container uses a YARP reverse proxy image for static file serving and API reverse-proxy. YARP natively supports HTTPS backends and service discovery, so API proxy requests work correctly across all deployment targets (Docker Compose, Azure App Service, etc.).

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.PublishAsStaticWebsitePolyglot``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.IResourceWithServiceDiscovery},System.String,System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Polyglot-compatible overload. All parameters are optional so the TS codegen wraps them in a single options object rather than positional args.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.PublishAsNodeServer``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a standalone Node.js server that runs a built artifact directly.
member.param: The JavaScript resource builder.
The relative path to the Node.js entry point to execute in the published container after the build completes, such as .output/server/index.mjs or build/index.js.
The relative path containing the built runtime files to copy into the published container. Defaults to the application root.

remarks.para: Use this method for frameworks that produce a Node.js server artifact during the build and recommend running that artifact directly in production rather than invoking a package manager script at runtime. The application source is still built using the configured package manager and build script; this method only changes the publish-time runtime container shape.
The container files source path is automatically set to so that only the built output directory is copied into the runtime container, not the full application source.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.PublishAsNpmScript``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Configures the JavaScript application to publish as a Node.js server that uses a package manager script at runtime.
member.param: The JavaScript resource builder.
The name of the package manager script to run in the published container. For example, start runs npm run start.
Optional arguments appended after the script name at runtime, such as -- --port "$PORT".

remarks.para: Use this method for frameworks where the production server depends on packages in node_modules at runtime. The resulting container includes the full application with production dependencies installed.

This method is appropriate for frameworks like Nuxt (where useAsyncData/useFetch requires the full Nitro environment), Remix (where react-router-serve is an npm dependency), and Astro SSR (where the built entry point imports unbundled @astrojs/* packages).
For frameworks that produce a self-contained server artifact that does not require node_modules, use instead for a smaller runtime image.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.AddViteApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a Vite app to the distributed application builder.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the Vite app.
The path to the directory containing the Vite app.
The name of the script that runs the Vite app. Defaults to "dev".

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.AddNextJsApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a Next.js app to the distributed application builder.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the Next.js app.
The path to the directory containing the Next.js app.
The name of the script that runs the Next.js dev server. Defaults to "dev".

remarks.para: This method configures the Next.js application for both local development and publishing. In run mode, it starts the Next.js dev server with the correct port binding. In publish mode, it generates a multi-stage Dockerfile using Next.js standalone output mode, which copies public/, .next/standalone/, and .next/static/ into a Node.js runtime container.
The Next.js application must have output: "standalone" configured in next.config.ts and a public/ directory (even if empty) for the published container to build correctly.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.DisableBuildValidation(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.NextJsAppResource})
member.summary: Disables deploy-time build validation checks for the Next.js application.
member.param: The resource builder.
member.remarks: By default, adds publish prerequisite steps that verify the Next.js configuration (e.g. that output: "standalone" is set). Use this method to suppress those checks when the configuration is set dynamically or via an external mechanism that cannot be detected by static file inspection.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithViteConfig(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.JavaScript.ViteAppResource},System.String)
member.summary: Configures the Vite app to use the specified Vite configuration file instead of the default resolution behavior.
member.param: The resource builder.
The path to the Vite configuration file. Relative to the Vite service project root.
member.remarks: Use this method to specify a specific Vite configuration file if you need to override the default Vite configuration resolution behavior.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithNpm``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Boolean,System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Configures the Node.js resource to use npm as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.
member.param: The NodeAppResource.
When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.
The install command itself passed to npm to install dependencies.
The command-line arguments passed to npm to install dependencies.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithBun``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Configures the JavaScript resource to use Bun as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.
member.param: The JavaScript application resource builder.
When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.
Additional command-line arguments passed to "bun install". When null, defaults are applied based on publish mode and lockfile presence.
member.remarks: Bun forwards script arguments without requiring the -- command separator, so this method configures the resource to omit it. When publishing and a bun lockfile (bun.lock or bun.lockb) is present, --frozen-lockfile is used by default. Publishing to a container requires Bun to be present in the build image. This method configures a Bun build image when one is not already specified. To use a specific Bun version, configure a custom build image (for example, oven/bun:<tag>) using .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithYarn``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Configures the Node.js resource to use yarn as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.
member.param: The NodeAppResource.
When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.
The command-line arguments passed to "yarn install".

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithPnpm``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Configures the Node.js resource to use pnpm as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.
member.param: The NodeAppResource.
When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.
The command-line arguments passed to "pnpm install".

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithBuildScript``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Adds a build script annotation to the resource builder using the specified command-line arguments.
member.typeparam: The type of JavaScript application resource being configured.
member.param: The resource builder to which the build script annotation will be added.
The name of the script to be executed when the resource is built.
An array of command-line arguments to use for the build script.
member.returns: The same resource builder instance with the build script annotation applied.
member.remarks: Use this method to specify custom build scripts for JavaScript application resources during deployment.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithRunScript``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Adds a run script annotation to the specified JavaScript application resource builder, specifying the script to execute and its arguments during run mode.
member.typeparam: The type of the JavaScript application resource being configured. Must inherit from JavaScriptAppResource.
member.param: The resource builder to which the run script annotation will be added.
The name of the script to be executed when the resource is run.
An array of arguments to pass to the script.
member.returns: The same resource builder instance with the run script annotation applied, enabling further configuration.
member.remarks: Use this method to specify a custom script and its arguments that should be executed when the resource is executed in RunMode.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithBrowserDebugger``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Configures a browser debugger for the JavaScript application resource, enabling browser-based debugging through a child resource that launches when the parent application is ready.
member.typeparam: The type of the JavaScript application resource.
member.param: The resource builder for the JavaScript application.
The browser to use for debugging. Defaults to "msedge". Supported values include "msedge" and "chrome".
member.returns: A reference to the for chaining additional configuration.
member.remarks: This method creates a child that waits for the parent JavaScript application to start, then launches a browser debug session targeting the parent's HTTP or HTTPS endpoint. The parent resource must have at least one HTTP or HTTPS endpoint configured.
member.exception: Thrown when the parent resource does not have an HTTP or HTTPS endpoint, or when the IDE extension does not support browser debugging.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.BuildServiceDiscoveryUrl(Aspire.Hosting.IResourceWithServiceDiscovery)
member.summary: Builds a service discovery URL for the given resource, preferring HTTPS when available. Mirrors the logic in YarpCluster.BuildEndpointUri.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.ValidateNextJsStandaloneOutput(System.String)
member.summary: Validates that the Next.js config file contains output: "standalone".

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.ResolveNodeVersion(System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Resolves the Node.js version to use for a project by checking common configuration files.
member.param: The working directory of the Node.js project.
The logger for diagnostic messages.
member.returns: The resolved Node.js major version number as a string.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.JavaScriptHostingExtensions.TryParseNodeVersion(System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse a Node.js version string and extract the major version number.
member.param: The version string to parse (e.g., "22", "v22.1.0", ">=20.12", "^18.0.0").
The extracted major version number as a string.
member.returns: True if the version was successfully parsed, false otherwise.
